headline: South Florida's Getting Major Moves This Week: Billionaire Real Estate, Luxury Towers, and Angel Investor Mega-Merger

stories.title: Pompano Beach's $2B Luxury Tower Boom Is Real
stories.summary: Developers are fr transforming Pompano's skyline with ultra-luxury towers from Related Group, W Hotels, Ritz-Carlton & more—Waldorf Astoria hitting vertical construction in May, Salato opening end of April. This wasn't even supposed to happen 10 years ago but now it's becoming South Florida's hottest beachfront play.
stories.takeaway: Pompano's lowkey becoming the new alternative to Miami—same luxury vibes, way more realistic inventory & pricing potential.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg Closes $170M Miami Mansion Deal—Biggest Ever in Miami-Dade
stories.summary: Meta CEO snagged a $170M waterfront estate on Indian Creek Island (aka Billionaire Bunker) on March 2, setting a new Miami-Dade county record. The nine-bedroom property features a secret library, jazz lounge, 60-foot pool—and it's still under construction.
stories.takeaway: Tech billionaires aren't just visiting anymore, they're literally buying up the entire 'Billionaire Bunker'—Zuckerberg, Bezos, Tom Brady all there now.

stories.title: Miami Angels & New World Angels Just Merged to Form Florida's Biggest Early-Stage Investor Network
stories.summary: Two South Florida angel groups combined on March 10 to create one statewide platform—Miami Angels (100+ companies funded since 2013) + New World Angels (60+ investments, $30M+ deployed) now pooling capital & expertise. They're using AI to handle a 5x jump in pitch volume.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's startup funding ecosystem just leveled up—bigger checks, more industry verticals (Healthcare, IT, Space, Fintech coming soon), better odds for founders.

stories.title: Google Cofounder Larry Page Drops $173.4M on Two Coconut Grove Estates
stories.summary: Late December/early January move: Larry Page (net worth ~$276B) just closed on two ultra-luxury waterfront properties in Coconut Grove for $173.4M combined—Banyan Ridge compound ($101.5M) + Casa Bahia ($71.9M). Lowkey his exit from California ahead of billionaire wealth tax.
stories.takeaway: The wealth migration from CA to FL is literally changing who owns South Florida's real estate—tech money is now the dominant buyer at the $100M+ level.

watchNext.title: South Florida Real Estate Market Continues to Break Records Amid Billionaire Gold Rush
watchNext.why: More details on how Zuckerberg & Page's mega deals are signaling where ultra-luxury capital is flowing in 2026—good read on neighborhoods & price trends.

watchNext.title: Why is South Florida's Tech Ecosystem Suddenly One of the Hottest in the Country?
watchNext.why: Palantir's Miami HQ announcement + angel merger news = South Florida startup scene is legit heating up. Worth understanding what's actually attracting founders & capital here.
